---
title: Parquet to PostgreSQL
---

`db2pq` now exposes a first-class Parquet-to-PostgreSQL path.

## Main helpers

- `pq_to_pg(table_name, schema, ...)` writes a Parquet file from the local
  repository into PostgreSQL.
- `pq_update_pg(table_name, schema, ...)` writes only when the Parquet source is
  newer than the destination PostgreSQL table.

## Typical example

```python
from db2pq import pq_update_pg

pq_update_pg(
    table_name="company",
    schema="comp",
    dbname="research",
)
```

## How the update check works

`pq_update_pg()` reads the Parquet file’s embedded `last_modified` metadata and
compares it with the destination PostgreSQL table comment.

If the Parquet file does not have parseable `last_modified` metadata, the
function will ask you to use `force=True` instead of guessing.

## Supporting helpers

- `pq_list_files()` lists available Parquet outputs by schema.
- `pq_last_modified()` inspects the embedded Parquet modification metadata.
- `pq_archive()`, `pq_restore()`, and `pq_remove()` manage active and archived
  Parquet files in the local repository.

## Related PostgreSQL helpers

- `db_to_pg()` copies a PostgreSQL table into another PostgreSQL database.
- `wrds_pg_to_pg()` copies a WRDS PostgreSQL table into PostgreSQL.
- `process_sql()` and `set_table_comment()` help manage the destination side.

## Related reference pages

- [pq_to_pg](reference/pq_to_pg.qmd)
- [pq_update_pg](reference/pq_update_pg.qmd)
- [pq_list_files](reference/pq_list_files.qmd)
- [pq_last_modified](reference/pq_last_modified.qmd)
- [pq_archive](reference/pq_archive.qmd)
- [pq_restore](reference/pq_restore.qmd)
- [pq_remove](reference/pq_remove.qmd)
- [db_to_pg](reference/db_to_pg.qmd)
- [wrds_pg_to_pg](reference/wrds_pg_to_pg.qmd)
